events.text: Each repository is a LUKS-encrypted BTRFS volume with its own Docker daemon and loopback subnet -- fully isolated. We create a two-gigabyte repository now.
Repository created. We list all repositories on the machine to confirm the new encrypted volume is mounted and ready.
Now we upload application files into the repository using rsync-based sync -- fast, incremental, and encrypted in transit.
We open a terminal session inside the repository to verify the uploaded files landed in the correct location on the encrypted volume.
Files in place. We start the repository's Docker services using the uploaded compose file -- in this case, an nginx web server.
We query the machine for running containers to confirm the web service is up, showing its image, status, memory usage, and assigned loopback IP.
Connecting into the repository's isolated environment and running docker ps shows that each repository has its own dedicated Docker daemon.
We stop the running containers while keeping the repository mounted -- a graceful service shutdown without touching the encrypted volume.
Now we fully tear down the repository -- stopping services, shutting down the isolated Docker daemon, and unmounting the LUKS-encrypted volume.
Finally, we delete the repository from the machine entirely, removing the encrypted volume. The config entry is retained for other machines.
